nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Grey was born in 1345, in Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Grey, was 45 and her mother, Avice Marmion, was 36. She married John Botetourt in 1359. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 30 January 1392, in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 47, and was buried in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud Greyが1345年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアロスエルフィールド グレイズで生まれた時，父John Greyは45歳，母Avice Marmionは36歳であった。1359年にJohn Botetourtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1392年1月30日にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン ハーコウルトで47歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン ハーコウルトにて埋葬された。

ko.Stanton, Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England,: 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 스트 존
ko.lifeSummary: Maud Grey(이)가 1345년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 로더필드 그리스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Grey(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Avice Marmion(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1359년 John Botetourt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1392년 1월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 하르쿠르트에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 하르쿠르트에 묻혔다.

ru.Stanton, Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England,: стантон ст джон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Grey родилась в 1345 г. в местности ротирфиелд грис, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Grey было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Avice Marmion, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Botetourt в 1359 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 30 января 1392 г. в местности стантон харкоурт, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 47 лет и была похоронена в местности стантон харкоурт, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud Grey nació en 1345, en Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Grey, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Avice Marmion, tenía 36 años. Se casó con John Botetourt en 1359. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 30 de enero de 1392, en Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultada en Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
